feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Ariba Buying and Invoicing
feed.subtitle: SAP Ariba Buying and Invoicing Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: You are not authorized to buy. Contact your administrator. - Guided Buying message while acting as
entry.content:
When trying to act as a user ,  if I add any catalog item to the cart in Guided buying , I do not see the checkout button. Instead a message is displayed "You are not authorized to buy. Contact your administrator." Is there any way , I can successfully create a Purchase requisition while acting as another user . The user does have there required groups ( like purchasing user etc. ).

 

Regards,

Saurav Agarwal

entry.title: Open Purchase Orders Migration
entry.content:
Hi Everyone - We’re working on an ECC to S/4 transformation project and our client has Ariba B&I realm connected to ECC today. The documents currently flow between the systems as explained below.

1) Create PR in Ariba -> Replicate PR to ECC -> Update ECC PR # in Ariba

2) Convert PR to PO in Ariba -> Replicate PO to ECC -> Update ECC PO # in Ariba

We need help to understand the best practices or approach we should follow to migrate the open PO’s to S/4 with this set up. For e.g do we need to convert open PRs and POs both as new documents in Ariba first and then replicate to S/4 or just convert the open POs from ECC to S/4 directly (which I think will cause issues in the integrations later if someone changes such POs after go live). Or what would be the right steps to migrate open POs in this case to make sure we don’t break anything.

Thank you all for the support.

entry.title: In GB, there is no validation for item category when creating ad hoc requestion
entry.content:
In GB, there is no validation for item category when creating ad hoc requestion using toggle for Goods/Services. Users are able to raise service requisition using Goods category and likewise. Can someone please help why CEME validation does not work in GB and what are the possible ways to resolve this.

SAP Ariba Procurement

entry.title: Wrong DUNS number synced to Procurement via sourcingnetworkid
entry.content:
Dear all,

we have the issue that every now and then the DUNS number set in Procurement gets overwritten from sourcing (the DUNS number in procuremt is required since the supplier validates it during POSR). I saw that we have a missmatch between transactionnetworkid &
sourcingnetworkid. Since sourcing was implemented before buying, sourcing uses a different SBN supplier than the one we have a TR with in Procurement. No attempt to align the IDs in Ariba worked yet.

Does maybe someone of you have suggestions on how this could be solved or the auto update be stoped?

Thank you a lot in andvance and best regards, Christoph

entry.title: I am looking for specific fields to use in Approval design to identify PR generated from quick quote
entry.content:
I am looking for specific fields to use in Approval design to identify PR generated from quick quote - zero touch. Can someone please help if it can handle via any existing field, or do we need any custom field for this? Please clarify. 
Requirement - Design approval flow to ignore a specific group when PR is created from Quick quotes - zero touch, kindly note PR is still treated as non-catalog after quick quote award.

SAP Ariba Buying and Invoicing

entry.title: "Quantity could not be Changed from to" error when submitting the Ariba Purchase requisition.
entry.content: "Quantity could not be Changed from to" error when submitting the Ariba Purchase requisition. Its integrated system with SAP ECC

entry.title: operational reporting for procurement synchronous api
entry.content:
Hallo Team/Experts.

I have the base URL for Operational Procurement API

https://openapi.au.cloud.ariba.com/api/procurement-reporting-view/v2/prod.

I would like frame the URL to fatch present invoice status from Ariba from Jan 2025 to Present. I am not aware to frame the URL for the requirement and need help if anyone can provide input to frame the URL which will be greatful as I am new working with API's.

Still need any additonal information I can provide.

Thanks

entry.title: Can Ariba send POs to Non-enabled suppliers via Email without enabling them
entry.content:
In Ariba Buying and Invoicing we would like to send Non-enabled suppliers POs online via the email in their supplier location, with the Preferred Ordering Method set as "Email", but we don't expect to quick enable them via the PO. We have turned off the "Allow Quick Enablement" option in the AN rule so suppliers will not be quick enabled via the PO email. The PO is failing to send to suppliers since we turned this option off. 

Our question is, does Ariba mandate to quick enable the supplier in order to send POs via email to them? Is it possible not to enable them but still being able to send POs via email to them on SAP Business Network? 

Thanks for your comments in advance.

entry.title: API/connector between SuccessFactor and Ariba Procurement
entry.content:
Dear to whom it concern. I'm a S-CSM located in Spain and one of my customers is asking to me if there is some API/Connector between SuccessFactor and Ariba Procurement? what they want to do is: At present in Teva, Successfactor is sending to Ariba, though Oracle GLB, the shared users data. Our plan is to descope Oracle for the user files. We are looking to take HR data directly from Successfactor and push it to Ariba. Can you help me to find if there is any standard API or any prepackaged solution or interface between success factor and Ariba for HR data?

With some internal support I answered the following: There is no API that I am aware of for this…once the customer begins using IAS for authentication and Ariba adopts that, it should take care of the entire user provisioning and user authentication. The customer could look at the SCIM API as a replacement, but there are some limitations with this API at the moment. Below you have the link about the SCIM API: https://help.sap.com/docs/ariba-apis/sap-ariba-scim-api-b3330550673e4208a0300f524f5b8104/sap-ariba-scim-api but it seems it doesn't work. So if one of you knows a similiar use case and can share it with me would be more than appreciated. Or if you know a customer who could do something on their own. Please let me know. Thanks

entry.title: Catalogue activation pre requisits and ANID
entry.content:
Hi all, 

Can some please share some knowledge regarding the pre requisits configuration to set on buyer´s side to activate supplier´s catalogue (external static and punch out) in Buyer Guided Buying?

I am very lost and yet have not clear understanding if ANID of supplier only must be maintained in Core Admin, supplier manager  or in addition to that TRR must be sent to supplier? Does supplier´s ANID in SLP has any impact?

Appreciate your help.

Thank you

entry.title: Web Methode to push invoice attachment to SAP Ariba B&I
entry.content:
Hallo Experts

I need some inputs/help.

There is an new requirement from one of our client that they are expecting to push invoice attachment separately through Web Service Call Method or through API. Need some insight how we can achieve their requirement and your input will help take it further.

Thanks in advance !!

entry.title: Ariba : Report containing PR and PO numbers
entry.content:
Hi All,

I would like to see a report generated from buyer login which contains PR and PO numbers that has ben created using Guided buying.

Can you please let me know how to get a report?

entry.title: Is there a way to prevent deletion of requisitions by submitter?
entry.content:
We are developing a solution in SAP Ariba Buying & Invoicing that uses the requestion as an intake requests.  Some will never be fully approved and sourced to PO, but they will be actioned on for intake purposes.  We would like to prevent the deletion of these requestions based on the value of a custom field (Intake Status - Completed). 

I tried created an approval node where item would move to that node based on the custom field status = completed and indicating no edits allowed and while that may prevent others from changing, the requestor could always edit and also always withdraw, then delete.  This is what we want to prevent. 

Would love to hear ideas

entry.title: Default a custom field value into a standard field
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

Is it possible to create a custom field cus_description on the Purchase Requisition and have its value automatically default into the standard Description field?

Thank you!

entry.title: Report for Paying Failed invoices
entry.content:
We've been monitoring Paying Failed invoices for years already and to get the failed reason, we are opening manually the paying failed invoices one by one. Is there a way that we can capture the list via Analytical Report?

Thank you.

entry.title: Ariba Collaboration Request Conditions
entry.content:
Can you please advise if Ariba has functionality that supports the below requirement?

a) Enforcing users to initiate a collaboration request based on specific criteria, such as when the purchase requisition (PR) amount is less than $5,000, or if the PR line is partial or non-catalog.

b) Restricting users from initiating a collaboration request when the PR based on custom fields selections

c) Displaying preferred vendors at the top of the supplier search results page when choosing supplier to collaborate

entry.title: Multi VAT on single invoice line
entry.content:
Hello,

We have suppliers who are posting invoice through Ariba Network for a simple service PO, these are PO with single line item for services. On Invoice posting , Suppliers are using multiple VAT components e.g. charging VAT 8.1 % and 2.6 %.

When we get these invoice in Ariba invoicing, there is no option to assign tax code on these split VAT lines. Instead, I just get an option to assign a VAT code on the total VAT, which is not accurate. Anyone else had this issue with Ariba invoicing? What can be done to assign appropriate VAT codes on individual split lines?

 

 

Ashish

entry.title: PR Integration via WebService in CIG configured site
entry.content:
Hello,

I would like to know if we can use Web Service integration together with CIG integration method.

I need to use Web Service integration to load Requisition from third party system.

For standard Ariba-ERP integration we use CIG now.

If that's not possible, would be great to know what are the options.

We would like to avoid replication of PR to S4 and then to Ariba.

#managedgateway #CIG #webservice

entry.title: Ariba Requisition Replication to S4
entry.content:
Hello,

I have requirement to create two separate processes in Ariba for the Purchase Requisition Creation.

We use CIG as integration method at the moment and only first point is configured.

1. Standard PR replication with Budget Check interface then PR approval in Ariba and PO generated from Ariba.

2. PR replication to S4 and further processing in S4 like approvals and PO.

Any advise how to configure will be appreciated.

#CIG #managedgateway

entry.title: Special handling line item-Ariba Network invoice
entry.content:
Hello,

We are facing issue with commodity code default on Ariba Network invoice when we add Special handling charge.

  • When I add a special handling line on network invoice- it loads in B&I with commodity 78, even though Application.Invoicing.SpecialHandlingCommodityCode parameter has been updated to pick commodity 78121603
  • But If I add a shipping line on network invoice – it loads in B&I correctly with commodity 7810, which matches with Application.Invoicing.ShippingCommodityCode parameter value 7810.
  • Both 7810 and 78121603 have same parent 78.

I asked Ariba support, why there is different behavior for Special handling vs shipping line, see below response.

Based on your initial request, we have updated the commodity code for handling 78121603. We did not make any changes to the Shipping aspect as it follows standard functionality. If we do not alter the commodity code in the parameter system, it will default to a four-digit code. Additionally, if we modify any other standard commodity code, it will attempt to align with the parapet commodity code specifically for the network invoice only.

Does this make sense? Anyone else faced similar issue?

Thanks

Ashish
